posts.name: Kent Comley
posts.cooked:
Hi guys, these wines are getting scarce around these parts. Going to a restaurant next week with a great list and a good selection of Barthods. Anyone have experience with these and suggestion for current drinking and why?
My initial thought is 08 or 09 Les Baudes.

2014 ghislaine barthod ‘aux beaux bruns’ 1er cru chambolle-musigny
2011 ghislaine barthod ‘aux beaux bruns’ 1er cru chambolle-musigny

I would go with the 2010 veroilles; it’s the most likely to be drinking well. 07 would probably be the best but I didn’t see one on the list.

posts.cooked: List has a nice selection

posts.name: Matthew King
posts.cooked: I’d go 08 Baudes. Bonnes Mares adjacent. 50 year old vines. Higher acid year suits her style. Gives it some lift.

posts.name: Rauno E N G el
posts.cooked: I’d be into some of those older LEAS in a flash :)!!

posts.name: Ryan Caughey
posts.cooked: ‘10 Veroilles. Because a ‘10 Gruenchers was phenomenal a month or two ago, and Veroilles is so darn good. Seems like a safer bet than an ‘08, as I found an ‘08 Barthod villages pretty darn acidic not that long ago (though still tasty!)

posts.name: Kent Comley
posts.cooked:
Rauno_E_NZ:

I’d be into some of those older LEAS in a flash :)!!

Yes some pretty great buys amongst these. Cellaring conditions are good so these should all be in really good nick.
2020 leeuwin estate ‘art series’ margaret river 340
2019 leeuwin estate ‘art series’ margaret river 320
2017 leeuwin estate ‘art series’ margaret river 300
1995 leeuwin estate ‘art series’ margaret river 245
1992 leeuwin estate ‘art series’ margaret river 245
1991 leeuwin estate ‘art series’ margaret river 245
1988 leeuwin estate ‘art series’ margaret river 240
1985 leeuwin estate ‘art series’ margaret river 240

posts.name: Jeremy Holmes
posts.cooked: Probably the '08. The '03 could be fun.

posts.name: Je@n-P!erre Prus@ck
posts.cooked:
ive had that 2003 aux beaux bruns…maybe 2 years ago. it was…very 2003. prune-y…no sense of place. barely could call it chambolle.

im a barthod fan and would steer you to the 2008 baudes or the 2010 veroilles.

i had a 2008 cras a few weeks ago…still felt like it needed another 3-5 years honestly.

posts.name: brodie thomson
posts.cooked:
If you can squeeze it in, I would absolutely buy the 1988 Ch. Magdelaine for $265.

Brodie

posts.name: Kent Comley
posts.cooked:
brodie_thomson:

If you can squeeze it in, I would absolutlely buy the 1988 Ch. Magdelaine for $265

Thanks Brodie, that does sound enticing but Christine tends to prefer to squeeze in Barolo and Burgundy!! I will try my luck.

posts.name: Greg Kahn
posts.cooked: I’d go for the 2009, personally and avoid 2003 and 2011 at all costs. A 2010 Fuees I had last year at the Bristol was so weird we ordered another bottle because we couldn’t drink it. Not flawed, just…weirdly minty.

posts.name: Tom Blach
posts.cooked: I’d be looking at the 2011s.

posts.name: Kent Comley
posts.cooked: My gut feel is the more structured Barthod style and cooler vintage might yield a sterner wine, but happy to be convinced otherwise. Can’t say that I have busted an 11 for a long time!

posts.name: Tom Blach
posts.cooked: It’s a very unstern vintage and Barthod does well in those-look at the lovely 92s, 97s and 2001s- but I can’t afford her wines any more so lack recent experience.

posts.name: Mich@el Ch@ng
posts.cooked:
Tom_Blach:

It’s a very unstern vintage and Barthod does well in those-look at the lovely 92s, 97s and 2001s- but I can’t afford her wines any more so lack recent experience.

My understanding is that 2011 barthods are quite bad.

posts.name: S_Yæt€s
posts.cooked: The JM Pillot 2007s on the list caught my eye including Clos St-Marc, any idea if those might have held up?

posts.name: Greg Kahn
posts.cooked:
Kent_Comley:

My gut feel is the more structured Barthod style and cooler vintage might yield a sterner wine, but happy to be convinced otherwise. Can’t say that I have busted an 11 for a long time!

11 Barthod is especially green.

posts.name: Greg Kahn
posts.cooked:
Stan_Y:

The JM Pillot 2007s on the list caught my eye including Clos St-Marc, any idea if those might have held up?

Very good if not premoxed; I have a few left.

posts.name: Wil Raley
posts.cooked: Anecdotally, I drank the 2010 Les Cras roughly a year ago. It started out beautiful, but closed down behind it’s structure about 45 minutes to an hour in, so take that for what it’s worth. I’d lean towards either 08 or 09 depending on your preference for vintage.

posts.name: Knut Sogner
posts.cooked: She and her husband made excellent 03s. My selections without a doubt. Own Cras in mag.

posts.name: Kent Comley
posts.cooked:
Stan_Y:

The JM Pillot 2007s on the list caught my eye including Clos St-Marc, any idea if those might have held up?

Stan,
I bought quite a few of these back in teh day and they were all good. Corton, Chevalier, Puligny Les Noyer Brets and various premier crus and never came across one that was poxed, but I did find at 10 to 12 yrs of age they were nicely mature for my palate, so they have all been consumed. For those who like a fully mature wine this would be worth a shot.

posts.name: S_Yæt€s
posts.cooked: Ah, been there done that . Those are priced at close to what I’d pay for current releases!

posts.name: Chris Barton
posts.cooked: Probably too late here but had 2014 Beaux Bruns a couple nights ago and after a couple hours it was firing. One of my favorite bottles of this year.

posts.name: D@vid K@ntr0witz
posts.cooked:
MChang:

My understanding is that 2011 barthods are quite bad.

Strange, according to some recent threads 2011 is apparently a terrific, consistent vintage.

posts.name: Kent Comley

David_K2:

Strange, according to some recent threads 2011 is apparently a terrific, consistent vintage

Love this. You guys burrow in like an Alabama tick

posts.name: Kent Comley
posts.cooked: Thanks, have a couple of these at home. One of teh few Barthods that I have in the cellar.

posts.name: Matthew King
posts.cooked: For what it’s worth, Beaux Bruns are always the earliest drinking 1er bottlings in a given vintage in my experience

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked: 09 Baudes or 10 Veroilles for me

posts.name: Kent Comley
posts.cooked:
William_Kelley:

09 Baudes or 10 Veroilles for me

Interesting…if I told you the list price of the 10 Veroilles is almost double the 09 Baudes, where would that leave you?

posts.name: Tom Blach
posts.cooked: There is no such thing. What is true is that there are many really lovely wines from this vintage, as from every other. Wine is not a boxing match and it is tiresome that some people, who clearly have no idea at all of wine’s true function, seem to believe otherwise.

posts.name: D@vid K@ntr0witz
posts.cooked: Relax. This is an internet wine forum and it was a joke.

posts.name: Kent Comley
posts.cooked:
Fully understood it was a joke, as was my comment. Fully relaxed here.
